pr.title: Matched HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress by rewriting the loop and pulling allocations out of the loop
pr.author: DavidWeaverAudio

summary: Matched HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress in src/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.c by reshaping the inner readdress loop and moving loop temporaries to outer scope. The patch replaced a count-decrementing do/while guarded by count > 0 with a for loop using a separate j index, kept count alive after the loop for p advancement, and changed one pointer increment into uintptr_t-based integer address arithmetic.

key_files.role: Only changed file; contains HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress loop and pointer arithmetic adjustments.

what_changed: In HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress, added outer-scope int count and int j declarations.
Changed count from a loop-local declaration inside while (i < arg0->priority) to assignment into the outer-scope count variable.
Replaced the inner if (count > 0) do { ... } while (--count != 0) with for (j = 0; j < count; j++) { ... }.
Preserved the per-entry updates: conditional addition of delta to *(u32*)(q + 0x14) when *(u16*)(q + 0x10) is nonzero, unconditional delta additions to q + 0x18 and q + 0x1C, and q += 0x40 per entry.
Changed p advancement from p += (count << 6) to p = (u8*) ((count << 6) + (uintptr_t) p), followed by the existing p += 0x10.
smart_moves: Used a separate loop index j so count remains unchanged after the inner loop, allowing the later p advancement to use the original count value directly.
Moved temporaries out of the loop body, which can influence register allocation and lifetime in ways needed for matching.
Rewrote pointer advancement through uintptr_t, a targeted way to force integer-style address arithmetic when direct pointer addition does not produce matching codegen.
Kept the semantic behavior of the readdressing loop intact while changing source shape for compiler output.
decomp_lessons: For matching, a decrementing do/while over count and an index-based for loop can compile differently even when they express similar traversal logic.
If a value is needed after iteration, avoiding mutation of that value inside the loop can be important for both clarity and matching.
Variable scope can materially affect generated code; pulling declarations out of nested loops is a valid matching tactic, not necessarily a semantic change.
Pointer arithmetic sometimes needs an explicit integer cast such as uintptr_t to match the original instruction selection or register flow.
This PR had no body text or review comments in the provided slice, so the matching rationale is inferred from the title and diff only.
naming_conventions: Preserved the existing HSD-style function name HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress.
Used short local names already typical in low-level decomp code: p and q for byte pointers, i and j for loop indices, count for entry count, delta for callback address delta.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Convert guarded do/while (--count != 0) loops into for (j = 0; j < count; j++) when the original binary appears to keep count stable.
Move local declarations such as count and j to outer scope to alter register allocation/lifetime.
Use uintptr_t in address calculations to coerce pointer arithmetic into integer arithmetic for matching.
Keep pointer stride constants visible: q += 0x40 for each entry and p += count << 6 plus 0x10 for each priority group.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
No issue/comment body was provided beyond the PR title, so reviewer intent and discussion are not available.
follow_up_queries: Find other HSD synth matching PRs that use uintptr_t casts for pointer arithmetic.
Find PRs that moved local declarations out of loops to affect register allocation.
Search for remaining unmatched functions in src/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.c.
Compare other loop rewrites from do/while to for loops in audio or baselib code.
